hypothesis: If chemical foundation models pre-trained on SMILES are used to generate molecular embeddings that are composition-weighted aggregated for formulations and concatenated with device variables, then neural-network models trained on these embeddings will predict battery-relevant properties across molecular, formulation, surface, and device scales more accurately and with superior out-of-distribution robustness than models using Morgan fingerprints, while retaining meaningful moiety–outcome correlations interpretable via Spearman's rho.
